  316. # [00:49] * Joins: benbyford (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  317. # [00:49] <benbyford> is this site really needed with the likes of stackoverflow and w3school etc?
  318. # [00:49] * Joins: sam__ (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  319. # [00:49] <@paul_irish> yes
  320. # [00:49] * Quits: test_wp (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  321. # [00:49] <apnerve> Definitely yes
  322. # [00:50] <benbyford> y?
  323. # [00:50] <apnerve> LOL.. how could you compare w3schools with this one? :-o
  324. # [00:50] * Quits: lucisferre (~lucisferr@d50-98-143-223.bchsia.telus.net) (Quit: This computer has gone to sleep)
  325. # [00:50] * Joins: fmoga (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  326. # [00:50] <benbyford> or the many many over websites ()just an example
  327. # [00:50] <benbyford> just wondering what the point was?
  328. # [00:51] * Quits: fmoga (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  329. # [00:51] <benbyford> as it wasn;'t clear from the video
  330. # [00:51] * Joins: weageoo (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  331. # [00:51] <tsingh> IMO. you get the befit of the industry leaders working together on this site. yet the site itself remains independent.
  332. # [00:51] <tsingh> run by the community
  333. # [00:51] <benbyford> soooo industry leaders are everywhere on teh internet
  334. # [00:52] * Quits: sam__ (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  335. # [00:52] * Joins: royw_ (~royw@2620:0:1cfe:18:694c:75d9:f39a:38f4)
  336. # [00:52] <benbyford> and where do you need them anyways? i know shed loads of talented people who dont have their name in lights
  337. # [00:52] * Joins: odde (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  338. # [00:52] <harryrf> I see no names listed on the site.
  339. # [00:52] <benbyford> just seems like alot of effort for .... what?
  340. # [00:52] * Quits: WaiSeto (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Quit: Page closed)
  341. # [00:52] <ChrisHeilmann> God I want to always take this connection with me. God bless sweden and its fibre optics cable network.
  342. # [00:53] * Quits: stevegill (~stevengil@sjfw1-a.adobe.com) (Quit: stevegill)
  343. # [00:53] <apnerve> benbyford: This is still an alpha
  344. # [00:53] <apnerve> Will take some time
  345. # [00:53] * Joins: Oshios (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  346. # [00:53] * Quits: Alien (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Quit: Page closed)
  347. # [00:53] <benbyford> yes....
  348. # [00:53] * Quits: elhoyos (~elhoyos@190.156.227.234) (Ping timeout: 244 seconds)
  349. # [00:53] * Quits: kewark_ (~krawek@nelug/developer/krawek) (Read error: Connection reset by peer)
  350. # [00:53] <benbyford> but for what?!!!
  351. # [00:53] <apnerve> and yes, there are many good resources scattered all over.. This is an attempt to put it at one place
  352. # [00:53] * Joins: krawek (~krawek@nelug/developer/krawek)
  353. # [00:53] <benbyford> thats my point, what are we building here??? more DOCS?!
  354. # [00:53] <apnerve> open community of developers building resources for a better web, regardless of brand, browser or platform
  355. # [00:54] * Quits: krawek (~krawek@nelug/developer/krawek) (Read error: Connection reset by peer)
  356. # [00:54] <apnerve> not "more" ... aggregating at one place for reference
  357. # [00:54] <ChrisHeilmann> benbyford: the point is that it is one resource where the companies building browsers and working on the standards have a centralised documentation.
  358. # [00:54] * Joins: krawek (~krawek@nelug/developer/krawek)
  359. # [00:54] * Quits: benbyford (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Quit: Page closed)
  360. # [00:54] * Joins: Kaelig (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  361. # [00:54] <ChrisHeilmann> rather than confusing people who want to learn with a mix of marketing and information
  362. # [00:54] * Joins: benbyford (~webchat@15.185.107.251)

  513. # [01:16] * tt is now known as Guest33112

  532. # [01:18] * Joins: Mike (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  533. # [01:18] <harryrf> Okay I thought I misunderstood something there for a minute, but all is well :)
  534. # [01:18] <jkomoros> Although ideally having a central place for documentation will help feed back into standardization discussions in the future
  535. # [01:19] * Joins: ShakeIt (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  536. # [01:19] <jkomoros> But basically no, this is documenting, not standardizing
  537. # [01:19] <royw_> Anyone working on Filter Effects?

  541. # [01:19] <paulofonseca> hi there!
  542. # [01:19] * Joins: netliot (~webchat@15.185.107.251)

  545. # [01:19] <paulofonseca> what is this all about?

  547. # [01:20] <@shepazu> royw_: I'm in the SVG WG, and I know a bit about filters
  548. # [01:20] * Quits: abb55 (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Quit: Page closed)
  549. # [01:20] * Parts: fof (~fof@unaffiliated/fof)
  550. # [01:20] * Quits: ender4021 (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  551. # [01:21] * Joins: ebarr (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  552. # [01:21] * Joins: ari_ (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  553. # [01:21] * Joins: ric (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  554. # [01:21] <royw_> shepazu: cool! I started hacking on them for the first time last night and I've got a few questions…are you guys looking at incorporating mouse location as a uniform?
  555. # [01:21] <jarek> shepazu: will SVG support namespaces other than XHTML and MathML?
  556. # [01:21] * shepazu changes topic to 'WebPlatform :: FAQ: http://docs.webplatform.org/wiki/WPD:FAQ :: logs: http://talk.webplatform.org/chatlogs :: bugs: https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/describecomponents.cgi?product=webplatform.org'
  557. # [01:22] * Joins: matthewriley (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  558. # [01:22] * Joins: tomkruk (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  559. # [01:22] <@shepazu> royw_: that's an interesting question
  560. # [01:22] <jarek> shepazu: I remember reading somewhere that the plan is to drop namespaces completely
  561. # [01:22] * Joins: Hardeep (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  562. # [01:22] * Quits: matthewriley (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  563. # [01:22] * Quits: ric (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  564. # [01:22] * Joins: wwkeyboard (~aaron@69.55.49.21)
  565. # [01:22] * Quits: tomkruk (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  566. # [01:22] <jarek> I mean SVG2
  567. # [01:22] * Quits: Hardeep (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  568. # [01:22] <royw_> shepazu: Was looking to do something where the effect originates where the mouse is and was unable to do it
  569. # [01:22] * Joins: sbditto85 (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  570. # [01:23] * Joins: scottrowe (scottrowe@nat/google/x-fzwpoglpmsyyaxbh)
  571. # [01:23] * Quits: sbditto85 (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  572. # [01:23] <@shepazu> I don't think we've talked about it, royw_, but I have thought about it… you should send a message to public-fx@w3.org asking for that with [filters] in the subject line
  573. # [01:23] * Quits: netliot (~webchat@15.185.107.251) (Client Quit)
  574. # [01:23] * Joins: Monk (~webchat@15.185.107.251)
  575. # [01:23] * Monk is now known as Guest87403
  576. # [01:23] <@shepazu> jarek: we aren't dropping namespaces, but we are reducing the need for them
  577. # [01:23] <royw_> shepazu: Also, It seems like you could also securely support control over where the sampling of the texture happens so you could support some effects like water ripples or magnification bubbles
  578. # [01:23] <royw_> shepazu: will do
  579. # [01:24] <dannybd> W3C is based in the Stata Center, right?
  580. # [01:24] <@shepazu> royw_: yeah, I think you're right

  1510. # [02:50] * Quits: srik (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  1511. # [02:50] * Quits: NACHO_ (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Ping timeout: 246 seconds)
  1512. # [02:50] <@Ryan_Lane> no. resources are delivered from the other domains

  1514. # [02:50] <WID4> Oh.
  1515. # [02:50] <jkomoros> Ryan_Lane: They don't have to do it anymore on most browsers for various reasons
  1516. # [02:50] <WID4> I see.
  1517. # [02:50] <@Ryan_Lane> the main site shows up normal
  1518. # [02:50] <@Ryan_Lane> jkomoros: ah
  1519. # [02:50] <WID4> But doesn't Google uses gstatic.com for that?
  1520. # [02:50] <WID4> No, wait, I'm mistaken.
  1521. # [02:50] <@Ryan_Lane> SPDY makes that unnecessary
  1522. # [02:51] <WID4> True.
  1523. # [02:51] <@Ryan_Lane> don't need the domain hacks
  1524. # [02:51] * Quits: test (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  1525. # [02:51] <@Ryan_Lane> it's not abnormal to break resources into different domains, though
  1526. # [02:51] * Joins: mrdunno (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1527. # [02:51] <WID4> Different domains?
  1528. # [02:51] <@Ryan_Lane> wikipedia has upload, bits, text, mobile, etc
  1529. # [02:51] <WID4> But why do that when you can regroup it into a single one?
  1530. # [02:52] * Quits: mrdunno (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  1531. # [02:52] <WID4> Oh yeah, for organisation, of course.
  1532. # [02:52] <@Ryan_Lane> each one is a different cache cluster
  1533. # [02:52] <WID4> Why not share it between all the domains?
  1534. # [02:52] <@Ryan_Lane> so, when you visit en.wikipedia.org, you get resources from wikipedia-lb.wikimedia.org, upload-lb.wikimedia.org and bits-lb.wikimedia.org
  1535. # [02:52] <@Ryan_Lane> yeah. that's exactly what's done
  1536. # [02:53] <@Ryan_Lane> en.wikipedia.org is a CNAME to wikipedia-lb.wikimedia.org
  1537. # [02:53] <WID4> Heh - and why don't you just put all the uploads on Wikimedia Commons?
  1538. # [02:53] <@Ryan_Lane> they are, for the most part
  1539. # [02:53] * Joins: Lima (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1540. # [02:53] <WID4> For the most part.
  1541. # [02:53] <@Ryan_Lane> but the domain they are all served from is upload.wikimedia.org
  1542. # [02:53] <WID4> But why not all?
  1543. # [02:53] <Lima> good site
  1544. # [02:53] <@Ryan_Lane> whether they are commons or not
  1545. # [02:53] <WID4> Wait, I think not all are served from upload.wikimedia.org
  1546. # [02:54] * Joins: matthew_ (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1547. # [02:54] * Quits: Lima (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  1548. # [02:54] <WID4> But I'm probably wrong..
  1549. # [02:54] <@Ryan_Lane> I can't really think of any that aren't
  1550. # [02:54] * matthew_ is now known as Guest64209
  1551. # [02:54] <WID4> Well, except the site-specific ones, of course.
  1552. # [02:54] * Quits: Guest64209 (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  1553. # [02:54] <@Ryan_Lane> those come from upload too
  1554. # [02:54] <WID4> I mean, not these uploaded to a site, but..
  1555. # [02:55] * Joins: Vicmar (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1556. # [02:55] <WID4> Aren't some hosted on bits.wikimedia.org?
  1557. # [02:55] <Vicmar> Hi
  1558. # [02:55] <@Ryan_Lane> WID4: that's interface images
  1559. # [02:55] <WID4> I mean, not content images, but...

  1592. # [03:01] <TehRealGawd> Hmm, do you all think that Javascript is going to become a prominent game progamming language in the future?
  1593. # [03:01] <WID4> Why not?
  1594. # [03:02] * Quits: hankbook (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Quit: Page closed)
  1595. # [03:02] <TehRealGawd> Idk I've seen people complaining because it takes forever to get to machine code
  1596. # [03:02] <TehRealGawd> much longer than native code or c++
  1597. # [03:02] * Joins: _23 (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1598. # [03:02] <WID4> I don't get what that means --
  1599. # [03:03] <WID4> JavaScript isn't compiled to machine code..
  1600. # [03:03] <WID4> So why would it ever get to machine code?
  1601. # [03:03] <TehRealGawd> Well from what I've read when javascript is ran in the browser the browser executes it into machine code
  1602. # [03:03] * Joins: j_njnj (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1603. # [03:03] <WID4> Well, of course..
  1604. # [03:03] * Joins: jb_ (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1605. # [03:03] <WID4> But what really matters is the speed.
  1606. # [03:04] <TehRealGawd> Exactly, and from articles and things that i've seen and researched it takes longer to get from javascript-machine code than native programming languages
  1607. # [03:04] <@shepazu> amirouche: we do plan to have translation and localization support, though it's not ready yet

  1790. # [03:18] * Quits: System_Resored (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Quit: Page closed)
  1791. # [03:18] <Ray___> Hello
  1792. # [03:19] <mVoid> It really shouldn't have backend
  1793. # [03:19] * Joins: NeoSwf (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1794. # [03:19] <WID4> mVoid: Why?
  1795. # [03:19] <TehRealGawd> alvincrespo, a few of us have asked that but haven't got a reply yet
  1796. # [03:19] * Quits: Ray___ (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  1797. # [03:19] <alvincrespo> I'm thinking we should have backend languages.
  1798. # [03:19] <WID4> Hey, the backend is important too for web development!
  1799. # [03:19] * Joins: benr (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1800. # [03:19] <TehRealGawd> Honestly I would love for backend to be added to fix the holes and things that are in them
  1801. # [03:20] * Quits: NeoSwf (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  1802. # [03:20] <TehRealGawd> I've seen and had many complaints myself about PHP
  1803. # [03:20] <mVoid> But this is focus on web standards for the client site, that would pollute the sites purpose
  1804. # [03:20] <skcin7> I agree, I also think backend should be added.
  1805. # [03:20] <mVoid> side*
  1806. # [03:20] * Quits: tapir (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Ping timeout: 256 seconds)
  1807. # [03:20] * Quits: LekisS (~tmenga@home.webrocks.net) (Quit: Linkinus - http://linkinus.com)
  1808. # [03:20] * Quits: benr (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  1809. # [03:20] <alvincrespo> I mean, if we really think about it - backend languages don't have a central location either
  1810. # [03:20] <alvincrespo> might be a big task to take on but it would make this site kickass
  1811. # [03:20] <TehRealGawd> alvincrespo, yeah back-ends are probably 10x less organized than front-ends are
  1812. # [03:21] <alvincrespo> especially if we look at it from the same approach when we document frontend code
  1813. # [03:21] <WID4> That's true.
  1814. # [03:21] * Joins: deeefff (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1815. # [03:21] * Joins: devongovett (~devongove@pcp049365pcs.pcv.reshall.calpoly.edu)
  1816. # [03:21] <WID4> While each back end language has its own documentation...
  1817. # [03:21] <WID4> Take Python or PHP, for example...
  1818. # [03:21] <WID4> Documentation for using these languages on the web is a bit more rare.
  1819. # [03:21] * Quits: shiver_ (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Ping timeout: 245 seconds)
  1820. # [03:21] <alvincrespo> Yes, definitely
  1821. # [03:21] * Quits: deeefff (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  1822. # [03:21] <skcin7> Backend languages can also be used for anything so other resources that may exist may not focus on web hosting. I think it would be good to have a resource for backend languages that are maintained by web developers.
  1823. # [03:21] <alvincrespo> the last time I did Ruby, just looking at their documentation was nasty
  1824. # [03:22] <TehRealGawd> i've been repelled from languages before just based on their documentation lol
  1825. # [03:22] <WID4> TehRealGawd: That's a real problem.
  1826. # [03:22] <WID4> Documentation is very important.
  1827. # [03:22] <alvincrespo> It might be alot to ask but, if we are definitely aiming to do web platforms then this would be something to definitely look at
  1828. # [03:22] <TehRealGawd> it's not fun trying to make things and having to wing it because you can't find a stable source.
  1829. # [03:22] * Joins: xabr (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  1830. # [03:22] <mVoid> Ofcourse it would be nice, but not on this site, this site's focus is client side

  2280. # [04:27] * Joins: Dekmabot (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  2281. # [04:27] * Joins: DarkSorcerer (~webchat@15.185.108.252)

  2283. # [04:28] * Quits: h43 (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Ping timeout: 256 seconds)

  2287. # [04:29] * Quits: fjc (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Quit: Page closed)
  2288. # [04:29] * Joins: TBIKC (~webchat@15.185.108.252)
  2289. # [04:29] * Quits: Guest4572 (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Quit: Page closed)

  2291. # [04:29] * Quits: esaruone (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  2292. # [04:30] <gluxon> Wanpeng: Open up the Buddy List.
  2293. # [04:30] <gluxon> Hit Accounts -> Manage Accounts
  2294. # [04:30] <ctoveloz[BR]> https://chrome.google.com/webstore/detail/mibbit-webchat/hbadbkkklnhamjjeagmknajgmbgcmnpi?hl=es
  2295. # [04:31] <gluxon> In the window that pops up, hit "Add..."
  2296. # [04:31] * Quits: TBIKC (~webchat@15.185.108.252) (Client Quit)
  2297. # [04:31] <gluxon> Then just select IRC in the "Protocol:" field and enter your auth info.
  2298. # [04:31] <gluxon> Easy peasy! :)
  2299. # [04:31] <ctoveloz[BR]> DarkSorcerer: eu man :)

  8999. # [09:43] <Ryan_Lane> this is the ganglia web frontend that comes from the ubuntu package in precise
  9000. # [09:43] <Ryan_Lane> it's a little old
  9001. # [09:43] <inkvizitor68sl> oh
  9002. # [09:44] <inkvizitor68sl> no heatmap, no aggregated graph
  9003. # [09:44] <Ryan_Lane> yeah
  9004. # [09:44] <desbenoit> France comes into the dance: http://frenchweb.fr/news-ce-qu-il-faut-savoir-ce-matin-34/80164
  9005. # [09:44] <Ryan_Lane> those are in the newer version
  9006. # [09:44] <Ryan_Lane> this'll be fun :)
  9007. # [09:44] <inkvizitor68sl> it looks like ~10 years ago )
  9008. # [09:45] * Joins: maknz (~maknz@unaffiliated/makx)
  9009. # [09:45] <Ryan_Lane> I get to watch the request graphs real time with location heat maps, too :)
  9010. # [09:45] <Ryan_Lane> from our front-end caches. heh
  9011. # [09:45] * Joins: mimir|on (~mimir@84.232.229.11)
  9012. # [09:46] * Quits: mimir|on (~mimir@84.232.229.11) (Client Quit)
  9013. # [09:47] * Joins: mimir|on (~mimir@84.232.229.11)
  9014. # [09:50] * Joins: timbl (~timbl@212.250.168.61)
  9015. # [09:51] <Ryan_Lane> 250+ req/s now
  9016. # [09:52] <mimir|on> congratulations to whomever had this idea!
  9017. # [09:53] <desbenoit> Ryan_Lane brace yourself, we're at a rate of a message per 5 seconds on twitter.
  9018. # [09:53] <Ryan_Lane> heh
  9019. # [09:54] <pzich> desbenoit: wait, it's not higher than that?
  9020. # [09:54] * Joins: k1rby_^ (~k1rby_^@ks37164.kimsufi.com)
  9021. # [09:54] <Ryan_Lane> we're at about the same level as the american peak
  9022. # [09:54] <Ryan_Lane> slightly higher
  9023. # [09:54] <desbenoit> pzich nope.
  9024. # [09:54] <Ryan_Lane> so, I'm not too worried. we should have worked out any launch pains then
  9025. # [09:54] <pzich> hmm, could've sworn I heard some other crazy stat, but that may have just been during a major event
  9026. # [09:55] <desbenoit> It's still early in europe, and the press is starting to discover the project.
  9027. # [09:55] * Quits: arnedebree (~arnedebre@195-240-16-75.ip.telfort.nl) (Quit: arnedebree)
  9028. # [09:55] <desbenoit> western europe, I mean.
  9029. # [09:55] <Ryan_Lane> yeah. this is the beginning of the frenzy
  9030. # [09:55] <Ryan_Lane> we'll see if it beats out the american peak by much
  9031. # [09:56] * Joins: ReklatsMasters (dmitrycvet@195.19.178.9)
  9032. # [09:56] <Ryan_Lane> miss times aren't increasing. still sub 1s
  9033. # [09:56] <Ryan_Lane> load continues to increase, though
  9034. # [09:57] <Ryan_Lane> that said, cpu utilization is still under 20%, and we're not even using 1/3 of the memory
  9035. # [09:57] <pzich> what was peak usage?
  9036. # [09:58] <Ryan_Lane> http://monitor.webplatform.org/ganglia/?m=load_one&r=day&s=descending&c=app&h=&sh=1&hc=4&z=small
  9037. # [09:58] <Ryan_Lane> you can see there
  9038. # [09:58] <astropirate_> i'm confsed
  9039. # [09:58] <astropirate_> webplatform reference section is empty :S
  9040. # [09:58] <Ryan_Lane> astropirate_: where?
  9041. # [09:58] <Ryan_Lane> link?
  9042. # [09:58] <astropirate_> http://docs.webplatform.org/wiki/javascript/objects
  9043. # [09:59] <Ryan_Lane> seems it was created today
  9044. # [09:59] <Ryan_Lane> http://docs.webplatform.org/w/index.php?title=javascript%2Fobjects&diff=6582&oldid=5837
  9045. # [10:00] * Joins: ejnahc (~ejnahc@unaffiliated/ejnahc)
  9046. # [10:02] * Quits: Lifesnoozer (~lsnoozer@83.223.19.160) (Remote host closed the connection)
  9047. # [10:04] * Quits: chaasof (~chatzilla@41.228.233.3) (Ping timeout: 240 seconds)
  9048. # [10:04] <mimir|on> Ryan_Lane: on what platform is the website hosted?
  9049. # [10:04] <Ryan_Lane> we're using hp cloud
  9050. # [10:04] <Ryan_Lane> under that we're using a fairly standard lamp stack
  9051. # [10:05] <Ryan_Lane> we're using fastly as the frontend cache
  9052. # [10:05] <mimir|on> cool, speed sis great
  9053. # [10:05] <mimir|on> *speed
  9054. # [10:05] <Ryan_Lane> mediawiki for the wiki, wordpress for the blog, questions2answers for the forum, qwebirc for the chat
  9055. # [10:05] <Ryan_Lane> thanks
  9056. # [10:05] * Quits: juancate (~juancate@unaffiliated/juancate) (Quit: leaving)
  9057. # [10:05] <Ryan_Lane> and we're using memcache for object caching
  9058. # [10:05] <Ryan_Lane> I'll be writing a blog post about this soon
  9059. # [10:06] <mimir|on> Ryan_Lane: that would be great!
  9060. # [10:06] <Ryan_Lane> we're using salt stack for configuration management, remote execution and deployment
  9061. # [10:06] <inkvizitor68sl> Server Apache/2.2.14 (Ubuntu)
  9062. # [10:06] <inkvizitor68sl> hehe
  9063. # [10:06] <Ryan_Lane> european peak is higher than US peak now :)
  9064. # [10:07] <Ryan_Lane> inkvizitor68sl: it's the version that comes with ubuntu lucid
  9065. # [10:07] * Parts: KindOne (KindOne@colchester-lug/silly-fool/donut)
  9066. # [10:07] <inkvizitor68sl> i mean "Apache"
  9067. # [10:07] <Ryan_Lane> meh. I guess I could have used nginx
  9068. # [10:08] <Ryan_Lane> the web server itself wouldn't really make that much of a difference :)
  9069. # [10:08] <Ryan_Lane> the vast majority of requests is served by the frontend cache
  9070. # [10:09] <mimir|on> Ryan_Lane: is there any varnish involved?
  9071. # [10:09] <Ryan_Lane> yes. our frontend cache is fastly, which uses ssd backed varnish
  9072. # [10:09] <Ryan_Lane> globally distributed
  9073. # [10:09] * Joins: ctoveloz[BR] (~Cristiano@190.2.97.172)

  9138. # [10:45] <chrismills> Hi all, Chris here from Opera/W3C/webplatform.org. If anyone has any questions about webplatform, I'd be more than happy to help.

  9140. # [10:46] <chrismills> desbenoit: why hello there ;-)
  9141. # [10:46] <mstalfoort> awesome chrismills
  9142. # [10:46] <chrismills> Or Bonjour, should I say...
  9143. # [10:46] * Quits: TehRealGawd (~TRG@adsl-98-86-38-29.tys.bellsouth.net) (Ping timeout: 244 seconds)
  9144. # [10:46] <chrismills> mstalfoort: hey ;-)
  9145. # [10:46] <Sandkorn> mmh, is there a bugtracker for the site?
  9146. # [10:47] <chrismills> desbenoit: if I teach everyone how to stop media wiki markup from breaking, you can give us some French lessons ;-)
  9147. # [10:47] <desbenoit> SandKorn it's there -> https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/
  9148. # [10:47] <Sandkorn> ty
  9149. # [10:48] <desbenoit> Chrismills we have a deal.
  9150. # [10:48] <chrismills> Sandkorn: yes - we use the W3C bug tracker, at http://www.w3.org/Bugs/. Use webplatform.org as the product name. There's a much more detailed article on fixing and filing web platform bugs at http://docs.webplatform.org/wiki/WPD:Filing_Bugs
  9151. # [10:48] * Joins: d2m (~Michael_H@178-191-74-39.adsl.highway.telekom.at)
  9152. # [10:48] <chrismills> desbenoit: cool ;-)

  9185. # [10:53] <chrismills> So I guess we will just have to wait until Ryan is awake again. He is based in the US, so really needs some sleep ;-)
  9186. # [10:54] <chrismills> He will have the IRC client issue in hand. I have also filed a bug to ask infrastructure to post the IRC connection details on the web chat page, so it is easier for people to just log on using their own client

  9189. # [10:56] <sz0ka> Okay nice, erm but the main reason why I joined was a bit more project specific.. I want to help you with your platform, is there anything i could do :)? Maybe translate something to german?
  9190. # [10:57] <sz0ka> I'm not that good in js or html but I've got some knowledge in python, c, chicken-scheme and so on..
  9191. # [10:57] <sz0ka> oh I forgot Java xD
  9192. # [10:57] <chrismills> sz0ka: well, we are looking to add translation facilities to the project later on; for the moment there are loads of other things we could use help on

  9263. # [11:49] <znowi> hi, guys. i think it's suffice to state the freenode channel name on the website, given the audience. on top of which your webchat is banned on freenode: Closing Link: 15.185.108.252 (*** Banned ).
  9264. # [11:50] <k1rby_^> znowi: the channel name is given : http://talk.webplatform.org/
  9265. # [11:50] <arkhi1> Maybe we should put the already known problems as the title of the chan? :)
  9266. # [11:50] <sonotos> hm good point, where to report bugs?
  9267. # [11:50] <Kevin3> It happens to me also.
  9268. # [11:51] <k1rby_^> sonotos: w3c bugzilla (see chan topic)
  9269. # [11:51] <arkhi1> sonotos: "at http://www.w3.org/Bugs/. Use webplatform.org as the product name"
  9270. # [11:51] <sonotos> arkhi1: thx
  9271. # [11:52] * Joins: antdillon (~ant@faun.canonical.com)
  9272. # [11:52] <znowi> k1rby_^: ah, somehow i bypassed this page. thanks.
  9273. # [11:53] * Joins: ReklatsMasters (dmitrycvet@195.19.178.148)
  9274. # [11:55] <markstickley> znowi: It's easily done. I don't think there's a link to that page on the site without going via the broken client page and clicking in the crumb trail.
  9275. # [11:55] * Joins: SoaDmaggot (~SoaDmaggo@46.44.189.17)
  9276. # [11:56] <znowi> yes, the chat link on the front page and the one on the docs wiki, both lead to http://talk.webplatform.org/chat/
  9277. # [11:58] <chrismills> k1rby_^: ah, would you say I need to add a link to make bug reporting more obvious?
  9278. # [11:58] <znowi> it's a good place to add a subtle "freenode #webplatform" note :)
  9279. # [11:58] * sz0ka listens to Diablo Swing Orchestra :: A Tapdancer's Dilema
  9280. # [11:58] <sonotos> arkhi1: would be infrastructure the right category for a broken link?
  9281. # [11:59] <sonotos> in the system, not in the content
  9282. # [11:59] <chrismills> znowl: I have filed a bug to ask the infrastructure guys to add the chat details to the chat page…but adding it somewhere else might be nice too
  9283. # [11:59] <sonotos> its allready on http://talk.webplatform.org/
  9284. # [12:00] <mstalfoort> chrismills, we should get this msg in a better spot in the wiki, so you dont have to repeat it several times
  9285. # [12:01] <chrismills> sonotos - depends where it is. If it is on a content page, such as a tutorial, then content but if it is on a page like the front page or the chat page, it's infrastrucxture
  9286. # [12:01] <chrismills> mstalfoort: I'll work on that
  9287. # [12:01] <mstalfoort> awesome
  9288. # [12:01] <sonotos> chrismills: thx
  9289. # [12:02] * Quits: Wanpeng (~One@124.127.60.42) (Quit: Leaving.)
  9290. # [12:03] * Joins: swidmann (~Miranda@host-82-135-30-117.customer.m-online.net)
  9291. # [12:04] * Joins: jerev (~jerev@196.176-242-81.adsl-dyn.isp.belgacom.be)
  9292. # [12:04] <znowi> i really like the logo. who made it?
  9293. # [12:05] <kranius> znowi: http://bl.ocks.org/3853405
  9294. # [12:05] <chrismills> znowi i can't remember exactly who it was. Google paid for a branding company to work on it
  9295. # [12:06] * Parts: pixolin (~pixolin@p57B83AAC.dip.t-dialin.net) ("Thank you!")
  9296. # [12:10] <arkhi1> chrismills: An idea would be to have a topic for the chan with one link to a static page listing all usefull links actually in the chan topic. Like a “Read this first” thingy?
  9297. # [12:11] <chrismills> arkhi: more useful than the huge number of links we have there already ;-)
  9298. # [12:11] <arkhi1> I would say yes. :p
  9299. # [12:12] <chrismills> ok - I'll try to change it in a moment, if I can
  9300. # [12:12] <arkhi1> Don’t forget to breathe as well.
  9301. # [12:14] * Quits: SoaDmaggot (~SoaDmaggo@46.44.189.17)
  9302. # [12:14] <znowi> kranius: thanks
  9303. # [12:15] <znowi> chrismills: was google also the originator of the project idea?
  9304. # [12:15] * Joins: Grephix (~Dennis@lb1.cluster.grephix.nl)
  9305. # [12:15] * Joins: YsenGrimm (~YsenGrimm@89.204.137.141)
  9306. # [12:16] <YsenGrimm> morning :D
  9307. # [12:16] * Joins: _Rainulf (~rainulf@unaffiliated/-rainulf/x-988124)
  9308. # [12:16] <chrismills> znowi: No - it was W3C who originated the idea; then it was us (Opera), Goog, Moz and MS who got on board early, then the others followed slightly later
  9309. # [12:17] <chrismills> YsenGrimm: hi ;-)
  9310. # [12:18] * Joins: Soarez (~Soarez@ec2-79-125-127-204.eu-west-1.compute.amazonaws.com)
  9311. # [12:19] * Joins: flurick (~flurick@banan.fulafisken.com)
  9312. # [12:22] <Kevin3> YsenGrimm, o/
  9313. # [12:23] * Parts: ReklatsMasters (dmitrycvet@195.19.178.148)
  9314. # [12:26] * Quits: Dapsol (~Dapsol@54192FBC.cm-5-2a.dynamic.ziggo.nl) (Quit: bye)
  9315. # [12:26] <kranius> chrismills: your employer is Opera ? what is your position there ?
  9316. # [12:27] <chrismills> kranius: I'm lead editor/writer for http://dev.opera.com, plus I do lots of developer outreach - coding demos, giving conference talks, etc. I'm Opera's representative on webplatform.org.
  9317. # [12:28] <kranius> nice, thanks
  9318. # [12:28] * Quits: rampr (~rampr@122.178.211.193) (Remote host closed the connection)
  9319. # [12:28] <chrismills> kranius: I am doing 50-70% of my work on webplatform.org at the moment, as I'm currently a W3C Fellow - brought on board to work on this stuff ;-)
  9320. # [12:29] <kranius> how does webplatform.org compare to, let's say developer.mozilla.org ?
  9321. # [12:30] <kranius> is it "more open", i.e. not "mozilla only"
  9322. # [12:30] <chrismills> All of the original content ("seed content") has been taken from the dev sites of Opera, Moz, Google, MS, Nokia, Facebook and others
  9323. # [12:30] <chrismills> We are working on repurposing our dev sites, so that they don't compete with webplatform
  9324. # [12:30] <chrismills> cs, well, that would be silly ;-)
  9325. # [12:30] <chrismills> cos*
  9326. # [12:30] <kranius> yes, that's what I thought too :)
  9327. # [12:31] <chrismills> So at Opera, we are putting all of our basic tutorials on webplatform
  9328. # [12:31] <chrismills> and keeping dev.opera.com as a place for higher level tutorials and proprietary stuff, like how to build Opera extensions, speed dial extensions, etc.
  9329. # [12:31] <kranius> kudos for the effort :>
  9330. # [12:31] <Soarez> word
  9331. # [12:32] <chrismills> thanks guys
  9332. # [12:32] <chrismills> there's a lot of work still to be done
  9333. # [12:32] <arkhi1> This is just so good… Congrats and thanks!
  9334. # [12:32] <chrismills> But we thought it made more sense to release it now and get the community's help, rather than just sit on it for another 6 months ;-)
  9335. # [12:32] <chrismills> arkhi1: cheers ;-)
  9336. # [12:33] <chrismills> Mozilla have given loads of their tutorial stuff over to the project too
  9337. # [12:33] <chrismills> I think they are planning to make MDN more about Moz proprietary stuff, like B2G, Jetpack, etc.
  9338. # [12:33] <arkhi1> would make a lot of sense
  9339. # [12:33] <kranius> indeed
  9340. # [12:34] <chrismills> Microsoft is the interesting one - they've imported about 3000 reference pages over to webplatform
  9341. # [12:35] <chrismills> that was a stressful script to write ;-)
  9342. # [12:35] <chrismills> kudos to those guys, seriously
  9343. # [12:37] <chrismills> HTML/CSS/JS
  9344. # [12:37] <chrismills> no silverlight stuff ;-)
  9345. # [12:37] <znowi> how does the importing procedure work?
  9346. # [12:37] <YsenGrimm> hopefully adobe will do the same :D
  9347. # [12:37] <YsenGrimm> no flash stuff
  9348. # [12:38] <chrismills> znowi A lot of it was done manually, for the tutorials, etc. But because the Ms content was so huge they wrote a big import script to throw it into the database. I don't know exactly what they used to do it...
  9349. # [12:38] <chrismills> Adobe have already donated some content, but I don't think we've used it yet
  9350. # [12:39] <chrismills> that stuff -> http://beta.theexpressiveweb.com/
  9351. # [12:39] * Joins: illoomn8 (~me@unaffiliated/krshna)
  9352. # [12:39] <chrismills> But seriously, the attitude is a pragmatic one. We will cover whatever web developers want to know about
  9353. # [12:39] * Joins: karlcow (~karl@nerval.la-grange.net)
  9354. # [12:39] <chrismills> If it is proprietary, then so be it.
  9355. # [12:39] <YsenGrimm> adobe did a lot of work on html5 like phongapl or brackets lately
  9356. # [12:40] <arkhi1> Whatever the direction, this is just a great move already.
  9357. # [12:40] <chrismills> But we will clearly mark that content as proprietary, and keep it in a separate section to the open standards stuff
  9358. # [12:40] <znowi> chrismills: neat. didn't know about this site.
  9359. # [12:40] <vldcnst> chrismills: slighty channel offtopic, but what is the best place to report a bug with Opera? (http://i.imgur.com/tXUUw.png)
  9360. # [12:40] <vldcnst> chrismills: and get some dev. assistance, of course.
  9361. # [12:41] * Quits: karlcow (~karl@nerval.la-grange.net) (Client Quit)
  9362. # [12:41] <chrismills> we can't really *not* include it, regardless sod our political leanings. Most web developers don't care about politics - they just want to get the job done.
  9363. # [12:41] * Joins: karlcow (~karl@nerval.la-grange.net)
  9364. # [12:41] <chrismills> YsenGrimm: yeah, they bought phone gap. I was chatting the Brian Leroux recently about it
  9365. # [12:41] <znowi> vldcnst :)
  9366. # [12:42] <chrismills> vldcnst: whoa! that sure is pretty ;-)
  9367. # [12:43] <chrismills> Please report bugs at https://bugs.opera.com/wizard/
  9368. # [12:43] <vldcnst> chrismills: ugh, okay.
  9369. # [12:44] <chrismills> vldcnst: what language Opera version are you using?
  9370. # [12:44] <vldcnst> english - standard install.
  9371. # [12:45] <chrismills> platform?
  9372. # [12:45] <vldcnst> windows 7, x64
  9373. # [12:46] <chrismills> hem, craziness ;-) We'll look at it
  9374. # [12:47] <vldcnst> the funny thing is, it looks like opera is going through multiple fonts - aka when I refresh, it's requesting the webfont again and rendering with a different one.
  9375. # [12:47] <vldcnst> eventually, after 4-5 tries, it will render somewhat readable, or even the correct font
  9376. # [12:48] * Quits: LekisS (~LekisS@2a01:e35:2f76:1460:517:3a75:e2fc:292a) (Quit: Linkinus - http://linkinus.com)
  9377. # [12:49] * Joins: LekisS (~LekisS@2a01:e35:2f76:1460:888b:4e1b:287c:ca3e)
  9378. # [12:50] <sowingsadness1> vldcnst do you have GlyphaLTStd65Bold font in system?
  9379. # [12:51] <vldcnst> sowingsadness1: negative
  9380. # [12:51] <YsenGrimm> ok need to go
  9381. # [12:51] <YsenGrimm> see ya
  9382. # [12:51] <Kevin3> webplatform.org don't pass the W3C tests. Embarrassing. :o
  9383. # [12:52] <chrismills> Kevin3: we've not done too badly, given that it is built upon a load of messy stuff like semantic media wiki
  9384. # [12:53] <chrismills> Kevin3 it was so hard to find a platform to build it on that was accessible enough to satisfy the W3C, and capable of handling that amount of traffic and documents.
  9385. # [12:53] <chrismills> semantic media wiki was the least worst option ;-)
  9386. # [12:54] <karlcow> vldcnst: there are a few bugs related to font on Win7 such as DSK-368378 and DSK-366808
  9387. # [12:55] <markstickley> Kevin3: There are 5 validation errors on the home page, 3 of which are related to youtube, I believe. So kind of not webplatform.org's fault
  9388. # [12:55] * Quits: YsenGrimm (~YsenGrimm@89.204.137.141) (Ping timeout: 246 seconds)
  9389. # [12:58] <vldcnst> karlcow: any suggested fixes in those/

  9400. # [13:09] <mcrss> Can anybody help me with a little javascript trouble? )
  9401. # [13:09] <fireglow> Hello
  9402. # [13:09] <mcrss> hi
  9403. # [13:12] * Joins: Gorkyman (~Gorkyman@unaffiliated/gorkyman)
  9404. # [13:13] * Joins: Peeter__ (uid6799@gateway/web/irccloud.com/x-jpxghmofpktyjyrg)
  9405. # [13:13] <Peeter__> Hello
  9406. # [13:13] <Peeter__> Seems like the DB is in a deadlock, cannot register a new user account on the site.
  9407. # [13:15] * Joins: dwoldrich (~chatzilla@184-76-96-137.war.clearwire-wmx.net)
  9408. # [13:15] <dwoldrich> huzzah!
  9409. # [13:16] <dwoldrich> haruphles
  9410. # [13:18] <mcrss> anybody help me pls
  9411. # [13:18] <mcrss> is there JS programmers?
  9412. # [13:18] <Grephix> sup?
  9413. # [13:19] * Joins: elhoyos (~elhoyos@190.156.227.234)
  9414. # [13:19] <Soarez> mcrss: how can we help you?
  9415. # [13:19] <Grephix> mcrss: whats the problem?
  9416. # [13:19] <mcrss> we know that we can create objects by constructor functions defining the prototype of this functions. And constructor returns new "class instance".
  9417. # [13:20] <mcrss> According to the fact that functions are objects I need to create a function by the Constructor function
  9418. # [13:20] <mcrss> sorry for my bad English )
  9419. # [13:20] <dwoldrich> here's my pattern, maybe it will help you
  9420. # [13:21] <dwoldrich> create a standard named function, that will be your constructor
  9421. # [13:21] <dwoldrich> refer to members of your class with "this.fieldName" in the constructor and in member methods
  9422. # [13:22] <dwoldrich> after your constructors is made, we need to build out the class
  9423. # [13:22] <dwoldrich> to do this, I typically do myFunction.prototype = {};
  9424. # [13:22] <dwoldrich> and then myFunction.prototype.constructor = myFunction;
  9425. # [13:23] <mstalfoort> dwoldrich, maybe create an example at jsfiddle.net ... that would help all of us
  9426. # [13:23] <dwoldrich> and then myFunction.prototype.field = 0, '', or null, depending on if it will be a numeric, string, or other object - in my constructor, I actually initialize the fields using this.fieldName =
  9427. # [13:23] <mcrss> this constructor will build an object
  9428. # [13:23] <carolyns> hey hey
  9429. # [13:24] <carolyns> http://docs.webplatform.org/wiki/html seems broken in terms of markup
  9430. # [13:24] <dwoldrich> uh
  9431. # [13:24] <dwoldrich> I'll see what I can do
  9432. # [13:24] <carolyns> formatting is wrong
  9433. # [13:24] <arkhi1> Bye bye to all web warriors around here… Have a safe day / night. :)
  9434. # [13:24] <carolyns> I'd do it myself but can't at the moment so I'm just pointing out :P
  9435. # [13:25] <mcrss> but i need to build a function by constructor
  9436. # [13:25] <mcrss> something like this:

  9935. # [15:29] * Joins: Alex__ (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-hyizesvhlledqjgr)
  9936. # [15:29] <agm114k2a> huh?
  9937. # [15:29] * Quits: Alex__ (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-hyizesvhlledqjgr) (Client Quit)
  9938. # [15:29] <Garbee> Is there a Roadmap of things to be done before the site comes out of Alpha? Or is it a "whenever we feel it is ready" kind of thing?
  9939. # [15:30] <@chrismills> agm114k2a: hey there! This is all about webplatform.org, the new W3C developer documentation site
  9940. # [15:30] <@chrismills> shepazu: morning Doug ;-)
  9941. # [15:30] * Quits: tets (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-awmkvposlmuoowsz) (Ping timeout: 240 seconds)
  9942. # [15:30] * Joins: pavlisliv (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-emaozohjpnnyyhuf)
  9943. # [15:30] <@shepazu> prastha: Apple chose not to have their logo on the footer, nothing political
  9944. # [15:30] <@chrismills> agm114k2a: if you are interested in web development, we've got a lot for you to see ;-)
  9945. # [15:31] <@shepazu> Garbee: the roadmap is largely captured in the bugtracker for now
  9946. # [15:31] <karlcow> shepazu: it should be in the FAQ given the number of times you are repeating it. :p or maybe better we should have a bot which answers each someone says Apple Logo on the same line.
  9947. # [15:31] * Quits: pavlisliv (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-emaozohjpnnyyhuf) (Client Quit)
  9948. # [15:31] <@chrismills> shepazu: hope you had a good sleep man. I've spent a long day so far doing nothing but writing interviews and answering questions on here, in the forum, etc. ;-)
  9949. # [15:31] * Joins: gtp_ (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-meisfxltgmwjhvvm)
  9950. # [15:32] * Quits: mobilejustin (~mobilejus@173.80.240.215) (Remote host closed the connection)
  9951. # [15:32] * Joins: danzik17 (~danzik17@164.55.254.106)
  9952. # [15:32] <@shepazu> Garbee, but beyond that, we do have features we plan on adding, and we will be documenting those
  9953. # [15:32] <agm114k2a> chrismills: i am, i am, and i recently read about webplatform in the news
  9954. # [15:32] <@shepazu> chrismills: how has this been received at Opera?
  9955. # [15:33] <agm114k2a> I'm starting up www.newsideo.com (sorry for the plug, unintentional) and always researching something
  9956. # [15:33] <@chrismills> cool. It's basically a coming together of W3C, Opera, Moz, MS, Goog, Apple and others, to create a killer new developer documentation site
  9957. # [15:33] <josie> Are you guys going to be incorporating most of the does on JavaScript from MDN? Or are you guys aiming at a different approach to the JS docs?
  9958. # [15:33] <@chrismills> agm114k2a: and better still - it's a wiki, so the whole community can contribute
  9959. # [15:33] * Joins: panaggio (~panaggio@201-42-114-78.dsl.telesp.net.br)
  9960. # [15:33] <josie> *docs
  9961. # [15:33] * Joins: volkmar_eigler (~volkmar_e@89.204.130.8)
  9962. # [15:33] <@chrismills> shepazu: very well
  9963. # [15:34] * Joins: nobodyiam (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-johmgfyzwmmkdmch)
  9964. # [15:34] <@shepazu> josie: we'll import some of the MDN JS articles, but there is room for a variety of approaches for different audiences
  9965. # [15:34] * Joins: fopt (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-azghegictwmwipfs)
  9966. # [15:34] <Garbee> ah, https://www.w3.org/Bugs/Public/buglist.cgi?product=webplatform.org
  9967. # [15:34] <@chrismills> shepazu: a few moans about how this will compete with dev.opera.com, but mostly just praise and happiness
  9968. # [15:34] * Parts: overra (~Adium@unaffiliated/overra)
  9969. # [15:34] <agm114k2a> I'll definitely contribute... since it seems to be more of a documentation site, is webplatform doing anything to standardize browsers at all? I mean, they're mostly there, sure, but I didn't know if this was a more of a push
  9970. # [15:34] * Joins: overra (~Adium@unaffiliated/overra)
  9971. # [15:34] * Quits: nobodyiam (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-johmgfyzwmmkdmch) (Client Quit)
  9972. # [15:34] * Joins: err404_ (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-fjpodxzpmhlofbrc)
  9973. # [15:35] <josie> agm114k2a: At the very least, it'll call out browsers that aren't consistent with the standards.
  9974. # [15:35] * Joins: mobilejustin (~mobilejus@173.80.240.215)
  9975. # [15:35] <@chrismills> agm114k2a: this site will definitely document the differences where they exist. And i think it might be a cool tool to use for helping us to fix those differences ;-)
  9976. # [15:36] <@shepazu> agm114k2a: well, one consequence of having all the docs in one place will be an increased awareness of non-interoperability, and perhaps increased public pressure to fix problems :)

  10077. # [15:56] <@chrismills> agm114k2a: it is up to the community to decide. With this site you've got tonnes of tutorials and reference articles, unlike stack overflow. And the forums are really for asking questions about webplatform, but if people want to use it for questions about their own work and stuff, then so be it. That is fine by us, and likely the solutionswill elicit more good documentation to add to the site
  10078. # [15:56] * Quits: hello_ (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-wxhlleloddgenyyw) (Client Quit)

  10080. # [15:56] <Garbee> eternicode, Those hot topics are relating to WebPlatform. PHP/GIT/RUBY things are not however. --On a personal thinking side I also think the forum might have roughly the same connotation as the chatroom. A place to help organize WebPlatform content and get things fixed, not for general support. That is a SO area.

  10102. # [15:59] <NickyZ> Here is a question for all the IRC experts. Garbee used my name in the chat and as such his name highlighted on my client. Is there a special command to do that?
  10103. # [15:59] <Garbee> chrismills, Yea. I had responded to a comment in the forum and tried to get that across. Someone thought it should expand *just* to compete with w3schools.
  10104. # [15:59] * Quits: Render6 (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-tgjxhcfsnjvucvcp) (Client Quit)
  10105. # [15:59] <Garbee> NickyZ, Nope, just saying someones name alerts them in almost all clients.
  10106. # [15:59] <@chrismills> NickyZ - you just use the name of the person you want to aim a comment to
  10107. # [15:59] <eternicode> NickyZ, nope, highlighting is a feature of your client. There should be settings to control it.
  10108. # [16:00] * Joins: dub_ (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-aotfarncrotwhwod)

  10111. # [16:00] <NickyZ> Awesome
  10112. # [16:00] <NickyZ> that was cool
  10113. # [16:00] <NickyZ> :)

  10116. # [16:00] <@chrismills> Garbee well, that is an unofficial aim of the project, but we wouldn't be as childish as that … would we?

  10121. # [16:01] <eternicode> The argument that mentioned "competeing with w3schools" was that they include some docs on PHP etc, so we should include documentation on backend stuff, too.

  10126. # [16:02] <eternicode> but I see crossing the line from frontend to backend taking us down a long, long (possibly dark) path.

  10128. # [16:02] * Quits: volkmar_eigler (~volkmar_e@89.204.130.8) (Remote host closed the connection)
  10129. # [16:03] <@shepazu> eternicode: we're not competing with W3Schools, we're just making our site the best we can
  10130. # [16:03] <Garbee> Yea, I see too many extra things personally going to backend stuff right now. We should focus on what it is set up for now and worry about expanding into those roads later on.
  10131. # [16:03] <eternicode> shepazu, understood, but that was the topic of an answer ;)
  10132. # [16:03] <@chrismills> Although you are right - to try to document all backend stuff would take us down a dark path - it would probably be unmaintainable, and dilute the quality of the site.
  10133. # [16:03] <NickyZ> shepazu: You must have that line on cmd+v

  10137. # [16:04] <@chrismills> Garbee: yup

  14427. # [23:46] <Tomato> Hey friends.
  14428. # [23:46] <sonotos> NickyZ: try "/msg nickserv help register" for all infos
  14429. # [23:46] <Scorpilus> paul_irish: k, thanks
  14430. # [23:47] <expert> hi guys
  14431. # [23:47] <@justinBeaudry> Syntax here is /msg /nickserv register <nick> <password> <email>
  14432. # [23:47] * Quits: expert (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-ibmbwhrbopwbxnon) (Client Quit)
  14433. # [23:47] * Quits: Lucas (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-kcgtiqsavvinbyuf) (Ping timeout: 260 seconds)
  14434. # [23:47] <NickyZ> with the / in from of nickserv?
  14435. # [23:47] <sonotos> NickyZ: no
  14436. # [23:48] * Joins: Tom__ (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-qedivtrzjaelyuxs)
  14437. # [23:48] * Joins: synopticcoder (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-fvblrpusrigjqpwh)
  14438. # [23:48] <@justinBeaudry> Sonotos is correct, my mistake.
  14439. # [23:48] * Quits: synopticcoder (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-fvblrpusrigjqpwh) (Client Quit)
  14440. # [23:48] * Joins: Brunis (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-uhhqqzvzvmincknp)
  14441. # [23:48] <mstalfoort> NickyZ, for more info you can have a look here http://freenode.net/faq.shtml#registering
  14442. # [23:48] <NickyZ> Thanks for the help
  14443. # [23:49] * Quits: Tom__ (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-qedivtrzjaelyuxs) (Client Quit)
  14444. # [23:49] * Joins: urlMulligan_ (webchat@gateway/web/qwebirc/webplatform.org/x-ilaymwzhkhwotmdi)
  14445. # [23:49] <sonotos> NickyZ: NickServ- Syntax: REGISTER <password> <email-address> Example: /msg NickServ REGISTER bar foo@bar.com
  14446. # [23:49] * Joins: danzik17 (~danzik17@ool-435606a9.dyn.optonline.net)
  14447. # [23:49] <sonotos> ok now it seams right
